name: instant-data — nightly backup CronJob stale (no successful run in >36h)
description:
Page when a backup CronJob in the instant-data namespace has not emitted a 'backup OK' success line for more than 36 hours (the schedule is 03:00 UTC daily, so a 36h gap = at least one missed night). Per-backup grouping (postgres-customers, mongodb, redis-provision) via FACET k8s_label_app_kubernetes_io_name. WARNING at 36h (one missed night) and CRITICAL at 60h (two missed nights — qualifies as a real durability incident per the runbook). Source: stdout from each backup CronJob, shipped via the NR log forwarder; success line emitted as '[<ts>] backup OK' by the CronJob shell scripts in infra/k8s/backups/*.yaml. Verified live by /Users/manassrivastava/.../infra/scripts/restore-drill.sh on 2026-05-20.

When this fires:
  1. Check `kubectl get cronjob -n instant-data` for `LAST SCHEDULE` and `SUSPEND` columns.
  2. Check `kubectl get jobs -n instant-data | grep <backup-name>` for failed Job runs.
  3. Pull the last failed pod's logs: `kubectl logs -n instant-data <pod>`.
  4. Cross-reference: `s3://instant-pg-backups/<prefix>/` — is the most recent object actually missing or only the log line?
  5. Re-trigger manually: `kubectl create job --from=cronjob/<name>-backup <name>-manual -n instant-data`.
  6. Full restore-drill once a real backup lands: `bash infra/scripts/restore-drill.sh`.
  See infra/BACKUP-RESTORE-RUNBOOK.md.

nrql.query: SELECT count(*) FROM Log WHERE k8s_namespace_name = 'instant-data' AND k8s_label_app_kubernetes_io_component = 'backup' AND message LIKE '%backup OK%' FACET k8s_label_app_kubernetes_io_name
